prompt: In a record producer script, how does an administrator reference a value the end user entered in a variable?
choices: producer.VARIABLE_NAME
gs.getVariable(VARIABLE_NAME) from the client browser session
g_scratchpad.VARIABLE_NAME evaluated on the target task table
current.getDisplayValue() for every unmapped catalog variable
explanation: Record producer scripts use producer.VARIABLE_NAME to read user-entered variable values and current.FIELD_NAME to set fields on the record being created.

prompt: A record producer must copy a user-entered variable into the short_description field of the incident it creates. Which script assignment is correct?
choices: current.short_description = producer.issue_summary;
producer.short_description = current.issue_summary;
g_form.setValue('short_description', RP.issue_summary);
gs.setProperty('short_description', producer.issue_summary);
explanation: current.FIELD_NAME references fields on the record being created, and producer.VARIABLE_NAME references the value entered by the user.

prompt: How can a record producer redirect a Service Portal user to a specific page after submission?
choices: Set producer.portal_redirect in the record producer Script field
Set current.redirect on the generated task record
Add a before business rule on the sc_req_item table
Configure a UI policy on the Catalog Item form
explanation: producer.portal_redirect controls redirect behavior in Service Portal, while producer.redirect handles the platform UI.

prompt: When a variable name on a record producer exactly matches a field name on the target table, what happens by default?
choices: The variable value automatically populates the matching field on the created record
The variable is ignored unless a business rule copies it explicitly
The record producer aborts because duplicate names are not allowed
The value is stored only on the sc_req_item variable editor, never the target field
explanation: A record producer variable with the same name as a target field populates that field directly when the record is created, provided the variable type matches the field type.

prompt: A catalog client script must hide a variable that is also set to mandatory without a value. What is the base platform behavior?
choices: The mandatory setting takes priority, so the variable remains visible until it has a value
The hide instruction always overrides mandatory and removes the variable
Both settings are ignored and the catalog item fails to load
The variable is hidden but silently submitted with a null value
explanation: In catalog client scripts the g_form priority order places Mandatory highest, so a mandatory variable without a value cannot be hidden or made read-only until it is populated.
